channel.description: Job listings for Public Affairs roles, scraped by Pol-Intel.

item.title: Head of Advocacy
item.description: The Opportunity ‍ Water UK is looking to recruit a Head of Advocacy, a high-profile leadership role with responsibility for shaping and delivering our national advocacy strategy. ‍ Reporting to the Director of Public Affairs, the role sitsat the heart of a rapidly growing, influential team and offers regular exposureto senior political figures, CEOs and FTSE-level industry leaders. ‍ Key Responsibilities ‍ Lead Water UK’s advocacy strategy, shaping and delivering campaigns that influence national policy and public debate Provide high-quality briefings for senior stakeholders, and represent Water UK externally, including at party conferences, parliamentary meetings and senior stakeholder roundtables Work closely with the CEO, Director of Public Affairs and Head of Public Affairs to ensure advocacy activity aligns with organisational priorities, and collaborate across teams to deliver consistent messaging and strategy Line-manage and develop an Advocacy Manager contributing to a high-performance culture ‍ Key Skills & Experience Required ‍ Significant experience in advocacy, government relations or similar senior political roles, with a proven track record of shaping and delivering advocacy or influencing strategies Strong political judgement and deep understanding of UK parliamentary processes, with prior knowledge of the water, environmental or infrastructure sectors being desirable (but not essential) Confident working with senior internal and external stakeholders at the highest level, with the ability to handle high expectations Line management or team leadership experience is highly desirable ‍ To apply ‍ We have partnered with Ellwood Atfield on the appointment ofthis role. If this role is of interest, please apply with your CV and Covering Letter via the link below - we welcome early applications. ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Policy Manager
item.description: An established trade within the financial and professional services sector is seeking a Policy Manager to support its commercial finance agenda. The organisation works closely with industry participants, policy makersand regulators to promote a responsible and well-functioning sector that supports UK businesses and the wider economy. With increasing focus on SME access to finance amid economic and regulatory change, this role offers the opportunity to contribute tohigh-profile policy discussions and engage directly with senior stakeholders across industry and government. Key Responsibilities ‍ Lead on policy issues affecting SME access to finance, including areas such as personal guarantees, credit data and sector-specific lending. Draft consultation responses, policy papers, briefings and board materials with minimal oversight. Support research projects, events and wider delivery of the commercial finance policy programme. Coordinate and contribute to member committees, including preparing agendas and minutes. Engage with government, regulators and industry stakeholders through meetings and roundtables. Build relationships with a broad member base, including banks and specialist lenders. Monitor regulatory and legislative developments, identifying implications and engagement opportunities. Produce member communications including updates, articles and presentations. Skills & Experience ‍ Excellent written skills and proven ability to write policy reports and consultation documents Previous experience in financial services, policy, government, regulation or a membership body environment. Confident engaging with senior stakeholders and representing organisational positions. Strong analytical and research skills, with the ability to interpret complex regulatory material. Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‍ Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. item.title: Business Development Manager
item.description: The Opportunity: ‍ We are partnering with a leading trade and accreditation body to recruit a Business Development Manager. This role focuses on expanding market share by promoting professional training programmes to employers with the capacity to train at scale. You’ll drive new business growth by positioning their brand and qualifications with large or high‑growth organisations, identifying fresh opportunities, and converting them into long‑term training partners. ‍ Key Responsibilities: ‍ Proactively identify and pursue opportunities to grow the number of clients engaging with our professional training programmes. Map and build relationships with organisations that have the potential to train significant numbers of trainees. Manage prospective client relationships from initial engagement through to onboarding Develop and deliver annual and quarterly account plans Work closely with internal teams and external stakeholders Plan and deliver high‑quality employer engagement and lead‑generation events. Track performance metrics to ensure targets are met and exceeded. ‍ Key Skills Required: ‍ Proven business development experience, ideally in a B2B environment is essential Experience working in practice, private equity, or corporate finance functions, or strong knowledge of professional services and related sectors is desirable. Strong negotiation and contract management skills. Demonstrated success in lead generation and sales conversion. Excellent skills in Word, Excel and PowerPoint, with confidence using CRM systems. A full driving licence and access to a vehicle. ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter forcorporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. item.title: Account Director / Senior Account Director (Technology)
item.description: The Organisation Our client is a fast-growing, independent communications agency specialising in technology, investment, and purpose-led organisations. Now in its fourth year, the agency has built a strong reputation for working with “meaningful” organisations across climate, femtech, DEI, financial inclusion, health, and education, alongside some of the most ambitious companies shaping the future of technology. The business has grown revenue by 30% over the last financial year. With a growing international footprint, including expansion into New York, the agency supports a diverse client base spanning start ups,scale ups, investors, and institutions across multiple global markets. Its approach is grounded in thoughtful storytelling, high-quality media engagement, and a genuine respect for journalism. The Opportunity This is a key hire within a high-performing and values-driven agency at a pivotal stage of growth. You will take ownership of key client relationships from the outset, leading delivery across accounts while managing and developing a team. Alongside client work, you will contribute to the broader direction of the business, including input into strategy, operations, and new business activity. The role offers significant scope for impact, responsibility, and progression within a collaborative and intellectually curious environment. Main Responsibilities Lead the strategic direction and delivery of multiple client accounts Build trusted, senior-level relationships with clients across sectors Develop and deliver media strategies that generate high-impact coverage Drive proactive media engagement and maintain strong journalist relationships Manage, mentor, and develop junior team members Contribute to business growth through new business and organic account development Support wider agency initiatives across operations and strategy Skills & Experience Demonstrable experience managing client relationships within a PR agency environment Strong background in technology or technology-adjacent communications Proven ability to secure high-quality, top-tier media coverage Experience leading and developing teams Excellent written and verbal communication skills Strong understanding of UK, US, and European media landscapes Interest in purpose, emerging technologies, including AI, and their broader impact Commercial awareness with the ability to identify growth opportunities Wider Benefits One-month paid sabbatical after four years’ service Flexible wellbeing support, including fitness or therapy subsidy Generous annual leave, including additional time over the Christmas period and a birthday day off Regular team activities and company-wide events Monthly development check-ins and access to external training Home office and technology provision Access to learning and wellbeing platforms Health-related benefits including eye care support Close To apply, please submit your CV via the link. For more information on communications roles at leadingagencies, contact Tasmin at Tasmin@ellwoodatfield.com . item.title: Public Affairs & Policy Manager (Wales) - Not-For-Profit (Health Sector)
item.description: About the Role ‍ Ellwood Atfield is partnering with a leading not-for-profit organisation in the health sector to appoint a Public Affairs & Policy Manager. This is a key role leading policy and external affairs activity in Wales, with collaboration across UK-wide teams. ‍ The successful candidate will lead public affairs strategy, manage stakeholder relationships, and provide expert political and policy advice to senior leadership. You will oversee a small team and play an important role in shaping the organisation’s external engagement and policy positioning. ‍ This role offers the opportunity to influence health policy, build strong stakeholder relationships, and contribute to meaningful impact across the healthcare system. ‍ ‍ Key Responsibilities ‍ You will lead public affairs and policy activity in Wales, providing strategic insight and advice to internal and external stakeholders. You will build and maintain strong relationships with policymakers, government bodies, and partners across the health sector, ensuring the organisation is positioned as a credible and influential voice. ‍ You will provide high-quality political intelligence, policy analysis, briefings, and advice to senior leaders. The role will involve overseeing policy projects, consultations, stakeholder engagement, and external communications activity, while managing and supporting a small team to deliver high-quality work. Working closely with colleagues across the UK, you will contribute to a joined-up external affairs strategy and help ensure the organisation responds effectively to developments in the political and policy environment. ‍ ‍ About You ‍ We are looking for someone with a strong understanding of the Welsh political, public affairs, and health policy environment. You will have experience providing policy and strategic advice to senior stakeholders and be confident in building trusted relationships with senior external partners. ‍ You will bring excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce high-quality briefings and policy documents. You will have experience managing complex workstreams and delivering projects to a high standard, as well as the ability to lead or support a team. Welsh language skills are desirable but not essential. ‍ ‍ ‍ How to Apply ‍ To apply, please submit your CV via Ellwood Atfield. Suitable applicants will be invited to an initial briefing call with our team to discuss the role in more detail. ‍ Closing date: Monday 27 April 2026 Early applications are strongly encouraged , as shortlisting and interviews will take place on a rolling basis. ‍ ‍

item.title: Director (Crisis)
item.description: ‍ The Organisation ‍ Our client is a well-established strategic communications consultancy. They work across a range of sectors, supporting clients through complex and often high-profile situations. ‍ The Opportunity ‍ This is a senior role within a growing and ambitious practice, offering the opportunity to shape and develop crisis and issues communications capabilities. The successful candidate will play a key role in leading client work, contributing to the broader growth of the practice, and defining areas of specialism within a generalist offer. ‍ Main Responsibilities ‍ Lead the delivery of crisis and issues communications programmes for a range of clients Provide senior strategic counsel on reputational risk, litigation, and high-profile issues Support the development and positioning of the crisis practice within the wider business Build strong, trusted relationships with clients and key external stakeholders Oversee and develop account teams, ensuring high-quality delivery Contribute to new business activity, including pitches and proposals Maintain high standards across client work, ensuring consistency and efficiency Mentor and support the development of junior colleagues ‍ Skills & Experience ‍ Strong experience in crisis, issues, and/or litigation communications (essential) Excellent understanding of the media landscape and handling of sensitive situations Proven ability to advise senior stakeholders in complex environments Demonstrated leadership capability with a collaborative approach Commercial awareness, with experience supporting business development activity Ability to operate with sound judgement in fast-paced, high-pressure situations ‍ Benefits ‍ Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package Flexible and hybrid working arrangements Generous leave and wellbeing support Structured learning and development opportunities Inclusive, collaborative culture with regular social and team activities ‍ To Apply ‍ To apply, please submit your CV via the link. For more information on communications roles, contact Tasmin at tasmin@ellwoodatfield.com . Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. item.title: Advocacy Manager
item.description: The Opportunity ‍ Water UK is looking to recruit an Advocacy Manager - acritical role supporting the organisation’s growing political engagement, campaign delivery and policy influencing work. ‍ This is a fast-paced and varied role at the centre of a nationally significant agenda. You will report to the new Head of Advocacy and work closely with senior colleagues to support high-profile campaigns and help shape Water UK’s political engagement. ‍ Key Responsibilities ‍ Monitor the political landscape and regulatory developments, providing timely analysis and advice Support the planning and delivery of advocacy campaigns across key strategic priorities, alongside the Head of Advocacy and Director of Public Affairs Coordinate engagement with senior stakeholders at the highest level, both internally and externally, and produce high-quality materials including speaking notes and stakeholder briefings Collaborate with the wider public affairs, policy, communications and member engagement teams to ensure coherent and aligned advocacy activity ‍ Key Skills & Experience Required ‍ Strong understanding of UK politics and parliamentary processes, with prior experience working in advocacy, public affairs or similar role Excellent written skills with the ability to produce clear and persuasive material, with strong attention to detail Proactive and able to manage multiple priorities with limited oversight ‍ To apply ‍ We have partnered with Ellwood Atfield on the appointment of this role. If this role is of interest, please apply with your CV and Covering Letter via the link below - we welcome early applications

item.title: Events Executive
item.description: About the organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield is delighted to have partnered with a respected trade association who are looking for an Events Executive on a permanent basis. The organisation is renowned for working alongside Government and business leaders to provide insights and advice around securing investment opportunities in Asia. The organisation work closely with its members to help businesses influence policy, connect with decision-makers and identify opportunities for growth. ‍ The opportunity ‍ The trade association are looking for a proactive Events Executive with a strong eye for detail to play a key role in delivering high impact events across the UK. This is an exciting opportunity for someone looking to advance their career in a fast-moving environment. In this role, you will be responsible for supporting end-to-end event planning to create experiences for members which will help strengthen stakeholder and business relationships. This includes supporting the planning and delivery of notable events such as high-level roundtables, conferences, networking opportunities and seminars. The role incorporates the full life cycle of event co-ordination, including concept development, planning and logistics, as well as marketing support and follow-up engagement. ‍ Key Responsibilities ‍ Support the development of event concepts, timelines, budgets, and planning documents to ensure smooth end to end delivery. Coordinate venue bookings, supplier negotiations, and vendor contracts to secure cost effective and high quality event services. Liaise with external partners to ensure all logistics including catering, equipment, and hospitality. Oversee administrative tasks such as delegate registration, speaker coordination, and preparation of event materials and resources. Provide onsite operational support to ensure events run smoothly and meet expected standards. Assist with event communications, including drafting invitations, managing mailing lists, and supporting promotional activities. Support post event follow up, including updates to systems, sending thank you notes, and consolidating feedback from participants. Contribute to post event reporting and evaluations to improve future event delivery and impact. ‍ Key Skills and Experience Required ‍ Strong written and verbal communication skills and confident in engaging with a range of stakeholders. Strong resilience and time management skills; able to work proactively. Proficient in Canva and Microsoft Office, whilst being comfortable with AI tools ‍ Benefits ‍ Salary banding £28,000 - £30,000 dependent on experience Hybrid working arrangement – 3 days in the London office ‍ How to apply ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. The deadline for applications is Thursday 7th May 2026, however we may close the advert earlier if we receive sufficient applications, so we would encourage you to apply sooner rather than later. ‍

item.title: Associate Director (Financial Services)
item.description: The Organisation ‍ Our client is a well-established, mid-sized communications agency with a global offering and a strong heritage in financial services. Known for its agile and collaborative culture, the agency has built a reputation for delivering high-quality, insight-led work, alongside industry recognition for its people-first approach. ‍ The Opportunity ‍ This is a pivotal role within a high-performing financial services team, acting as the number two to the Head of Financial Services. You will play a central role in shaping client strategy, leading delivery across key accounts, and supporting the continued growth of the practice within a nimble and ambitious agency environment. ‍ Main Responsibilities ‍ Lead the strategic direction and delivery of integrated communications programmes for financial services clients Act as a senior counsel to clients, building trusted and long-term relationships Support and deputise for the Head of Financial Services as required Drive new business activity, including contributing to pitches and identifying growth opportunities Oversee and mentor junior team members, fostering a high-performing and supportive team culture Ensure consistently high standards of delivery across multiple accounts Collaborate across the wider agency to deliver integrated, multi-market campaigns Skills & Experience ‍ Significant experience within a communications agency, with a focus on financial services Strong understanding of the financial services landscape, including regulatory and reputational considerations Proven ability to lead client relationships and provide senior strategic counsel Experience in managing and developing high-performing teams Track record of contributing to business development and agency growth Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal Ability to operate effectively in a fast-paced, agile environment Close ‍ To apply, please submit your CV via the link. ‍ For more information on communications roles, contact Tasmin at tasmin@ellwoodatfield.com. ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. item.title: Digital Senior Account Manager
item.description: The Organisation ‍ Our client is a fast-growing strategic advisory firm operating at the intersection of communications, policy and reputation. With an international footprint, the firm supports senior leaders across government, business and high-profile organisations navigating complex, high-stakes environments. The team is known for its discreet, insight-led approach, combining geopolitical awareness with clear strategic execution. ‍ The Opportunity ‍ This is an exciting opportunity for a digitally focused communications professional to join a high-performing and entrepreneurial team. You will play a key role in delivering integrated campaigns and digital strategies for clients operating in complex and often high-profile contexts. The role offers significant international exposure, opportunity for regular travel and the chance to work on high-impact mandates with senior stakeholders. ‍ Main Responsibilities ‍ Plan and execute digital communications strategies across sensitive, high-profile client accounts Manage day-to-day social media activity, including drafting, scheduling and publishing content for senior individuals and organisations Develop digital narratives that support reputation management, public positioning and campaign objectives Monitor online conversations, media coverage and stakeholder sentiment, providing real-time insight and recommendations Support crisis and issues management through rapid-response digital content and messaging Conduct digital and stakeholder research, including audience mapping and platform analysis, to inform strategy Analyse campaign performance and produce insight-led reports to refine and optimise activity Work closely with colleagues across strategy, research and public affairs to deliver integrated client programmes Provide hands-on support to senior clients, ensuring digital output is accurate, timely and aligned with wider communications objectives Monitor political, regulatory and media landscapes to inform campaign activity ‍ Skills & Experience ‍ Experience in digital communications within an agency, in-house or public sector environment Strong understanding of social media, digital campaigning and online engagement strategies Ability to operate in fast-paced, high-pressure environments with sound judgement Experience supporting senior stakeholders or working on campaigns involving complex or sensitive issues Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate data into actionable insights Interest in geopolitics and international affairs Excellent written and verbal communication skills Likely to have 3–5 years’ relevant experience – this will not be suitable for someone more experienced and there is no flexibility here ‍ To Apply ‍ To apply, please submit your CV via the link. ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. item.title: Head of Public Policy & Research
item.description: The organisation ‍ People’s Partnership is a leading financial services organisation, responsible for supporting more than seven million savers across the UK, with over £40bn Assets Under Management (AUM). It plays a significant role in the pensions landscape, working to improve financial outcomes and deliver long-term value for its members. The organisation is recognised for it strong commitment to innovation, customer focus and driving positive change across the industry. ‍ ‍ The opportunity ‍ People’s Partnership is seeking a Head of Public Policy& Research to lead its thought leadership, research, campaigns and public policy agenda at a senior level. This role will shape how the organisation influences government, industry and key stakeholders, ensuring its voice is clear, credible and impactful across the pensions and financial services landscape. Working closely with senior leadership, including the Head of Public Affairs, Head of Policy and executive team, the Head of Public Policy & Research will set the strategic direction for research, corporate narrative, campaigns and external positioning. ‍ ‍ Main responsibilities ‍ Lead the development and delivery of a strategic thought leadership and policy research programme aligned to organisational priorities Set and own the corporate policy narrative, ensuring clear and consistent positioning across external policy audiences and internal stakeholders Oversee the commissioning and delivery of high-quality research and insight to support policy development and industry influence Build and maintain senior relationships across industry, government, think tanks and partner organisations Lead and develop a high-performing team, ensuring delivery of impactful campaigns, content and public policy outputs. ‍ Key skills and experience ‍ Senior experience in policy, research, public affairs or corporate affairs within financial services, government, a think tank or membership organisation Strong track record of developing thought leadership and influencing policy and industry debate Experience commissioning and translating research into clear, compelling narratives for external audiences Excellent stakeholder management skills, with the ability to engage and influence at senior level Strong communication skills, with experience producing high-quality briefings, reports and strategic content. ‍ ‍ ‍ Application Please apply below by Monday 18th May. ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Faculty Communications Manager (Humanities and Social Sciences)
item.description: The organisation: ‍ Ellwood Atfield is thrilled to partner with Queen Mary University of London, a prestigious Russell Group University located in the heart of East London. Ranked 31st in the world for the quality of their research, and with a long-standing history of commitment to social justice and academic excellence, Queen Mary is consistently ranked among the top universities in the UK and globally. ‍ With a diverse and inclusive community of students and staff from over 170 countries, the University fosters an environment that encourages innovation, collaboration, and critical thinking across a very wide range of disciplines. ‍ ‍ The opportunity: ‍ The role of Faculty Communications Manager (Humanities and Social Sciences) sits within the central Communications team and serves as the primary communications lead for the Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences. ‍ As one of three Faculty Communications Managers—each aligned with one of the University’s faculties—this role is responsible for developing and delivering strategic internal and external communications for the Faculty of Humanities and Social Sciences. Working closely with Faculty leadership, academic and professional services staff, the postholder supports the University’s broader goals of increasing awareness and reputation, driving student recruitment, and enhancing engagement among staff and students. ‍ The Faculty Communications Manager (Humanities and Social Sciences) is the lead contact for communications across the Faculty and works in close partnership with the Faculty Marketing Manager to be a point of contact to the central marketing and communications services. The role is supported by the central Communications team and directly line manages a team of two communications officers. ‍ Main Responsibilities ‍ Lead and deliver integrated internal and external communications strategies for the Faculty, supporting reputation, engagement (internal and external) and student recruitment objectives. Act as the principal communications adviser to Faculty leadership teams, providing expert guidance on media relations, campaigns, stakeholder communications and reputation management. Develop high-quality content across multiple channels, including press releases, digital platforms, social media, newsletters and leadership communications, ensuring consistency of brand and messaging. Monitor, evaluate and report on communications activity and campaign performance, using insight and analytics to inform future strategy and continuous improvement. ‍ Key Skills and Experience required: ‍ Significant experience of developing and implementing successful communications activities and campaigns (including media relations activities). Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with experience translating complex subject matter into engaging and accessible content for diverse audiences. Proven experience of coordinating integrated communications across traditional and digital platforms to meet marketing and communications goals. Comfortable working alongside senior leadership teams and or Boards/Committees. Experience of working in within higher education or a complex organisation. Experience of matrix managing and directly line managing others Proven ability to meet tight deadlines ‍ Benefits: ‍ Salary banding: £54,617 – £60,901, dependent on experience* Hybrid working (3 days per week in the office) Office based in London (E1 4UJ) 30 days holiday plus bank holidays *Please note that Queen Mary advertise salaries on a range to indicate the trajectory of progression that can be made. Appointments are usually made at the start of the salary range. The Queen Mary salary structure includes automatic pay progression within the published grades, subject to service, funding and performance. In addition to this, there are performance related annual pay review schemes in place. ‍ How to apply: ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. The deadline for applications is Tuesday 19 th May 2026, however we may close the advert earlier if we receive sufficient applications, so we would encourage you to apply sooner rather than later. Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. item.title: Senior Director of Executive Communications
item.description: ‍ Role Purpose Lead and shape the executive voice of a global organisation, driving strategic communications and employee engagement across Europe. ‍ The Senior Director, Executive Communications is responsible for shaping, protecting and amplifying the voice of the CEO and senior leadership across Europe. This role sits at the intersection of strategy, storytelling, employee engagement and digital communications, translating enterprise priorities into clear, compelling narratives that drive alignment, trust and performance. ‍ The role is both strategic and highly operational, owning end to end delivery of executive communications, from narrative development and drafting through to digital execution, sequencing and distribution. ‍ Key Responsibilities ‍ Set and steward the CEO and enterprise narrative, ensuring clarity, consistency and relevance across all channels and moments that matter. Draft and edit high‑stakes communications on behalf of the CEO and ELT, including organisational announcements, leadership notes, speeches, townhalls, blogs and event messaging. Act as a trusted advisor to senior leaders, providing counsel on tone, timing, positioning and stakeholder impact. Lead enterprise‑wide employee communications, translating strategy, transformation and performance into messages that resonate with colleagues. Own leadership‑led employee moments such as town halls, leadership updates and milestone communications, ensuring they are engaging, data‑driven and authentic. Partner closely with People, Strategy and Leadership teams to reinforce culture, purpose and change. Drive a digital‑first approach to executive and employee communications, using platforms such as Engage, intranet, video and social to extend reach and relevance. Modernise executive communications through smart use of visuals, links, data, multimedia and new formats, ensuring content is concise, scannable and shareable. Ensure messages are adapted appropriately for different channels while maintaining a single narrative spine. Own the end‑to‑end communications flow for executive and enterprise priorities, including sequencing, timing and coordination across internal, external and digital channels. Work seamlessly with Corporate, Product and Market Communications teams to ensure alignment and avoid fragmentation. Partner with global counterparts to localise and execute global narratives effectively in Europe. Act as a final editorial gatekeeper for executive communications, ensuring accuracy, consistency and readiness before distribution. Manage sensitive, confidential communications with discretion and sound judgement. Anticipate reputational risk and advise on mitigation through proactive messaging. Key Skills and Experience ‍ Extensive experience in executive, employee or enterprise communications within a complex, matrixed organisation. Exceptional writing and editing skills, with the ability to shift seamlessly between strategic narrative and sharp, concise execution. Strong understanding of digital communications and channel strategy, with a track record of modernising leadership communications. High emotional intelligence and confidence operating with senior leaders. Proven ability to manage multiple high‑stakes workstreams under pressure without losing quality or judgement. ‍ Application ‍ To apply, please do so below, or contact Jamie Ferguson at jamie.f@ellwoodatfield.com. ‍

item.title: Policy and Public Affairs Lead (Wales)
item.description: The organisation Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with a leading health charitythat plays a prominent role in shaping policy and delivering high-profilecampaigns to improve access to diagnosis, treatment and support for patientsacross the UK. The opportunity Our client is looking for an experienced Policy and Public Affairs Lead to leadthe development and communication of evidence-based policy positions acrossWales. In this role, you will play a central part in shaping and deliveringinfluencing strategies, while building and maintaining strong relationshipswith stakeholders across the Welsh Government and wider health sector. You will work closely with researchers, healthcareprofessionals and external partners to amplify policy and campaign activity,ensuring the charity’s priorities are informed by robust evidence and capableof driving meaningful change. This is an exciting opportunity to join a highlyregarded organisation at a time of significant policy focus and campaigningactivity across Wales. ‍ Main responsibilities Work closely with colleagues across the organisation to ensure alignment on policy and campaign priorities. Draft high-quality policy materials including briefings, consultation responses, reports and other written outputs on key policy areas. Ensure policy development is backed by a strong evidence base, informed by healthcare professionals and patient insight across Wales. Monitor the political and healthcare landscape in Wales to identify emerging risks, opportunities and influencing priorities. Collaborate with external organisations on areas of mutual interest to maximise campaign reach and impact. Work closely with public affairs colleagues to identify and deliver activities that support the charity’s influencing objectives. Build and maintain effective relationships with policymakers, healthcare leaders, clinicians and external partners across Wales. Represent the charity at key events and conferences, acting as a source of expertise on the Welsh political and healthcare landscape. Support the press and PR team to ensure policy and public affairs priorities are reflected in media activity and opportunities. ‍ Key skills and experience required Experience developing and implementing policy and influencing strategies within a Welsh political environment. Strong ability to utilise data and evidence, including patient insight, to support policy development and influencing activity. Proven track record of delivering public affairs activity across Wales, with the ability to influence senior external stakeholders. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to represent the charity externally and communicate complex policy issues clearly. Strong project management skills, with the ability to work independently while managing multiple priorities and stakeholders. ‍ Benefits ‍ Salary banding between £36,500 - £38,000 Hybrid working, with 3 days per week in the Cardiff office 25 days annual leave, plus an additional day for each full year worked (up to 30 days) ‍ How to apply ‍ To apply, please submit your CV via the link below. ‍ The deadline for applications is Tuesday 26th May 2026. However, we may close the advert earlier if we receive a high volume of applications, so we would encourage you to apply sooner rather than later. ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Senior Policy Manager
item.description: The organisation ‍ London & Partners is the growth agency for London, working to support the city’s economy, attract investment, drive innovation and help businesses scale. Working across high-growth sectors and in partnership with business, government and industry, London & Partners plays a central role in promoting London as a leading global city for investment, talent and innovation. Its work focuses on building a more sustainable, resilient and inclusive economy for the capital. ‍ The opportunity ‍ London & Partners is seeking a Senior Policy Manager to play a leading role in shaping policy and thought leadership focused on London’s economic growth. Sitting within the Policy and Impact Unit, the Senior Policy Manager will develop insight-led policy ideas and market interventions that support growth sectors, innovation and business development across the capital. The role offers the opportunity to work on high-profile projects spanning areas such as innovation, climate, life sciences, AI and business growth, while engaging closely with senior stakeholders across government, industry and the wider London ecosystem. ‍ Main responsibilities ‍ Develop policy insights, thought leadership and market-focused recommendations to support London’s economic growth and high-growth sectors Lead and manage complex policy and strategy projects, ensuring delivery to agreed time, budget and quality standards Analyse economic, sector and policy trends to identify opportunities, barriers and practical solutions Build and maintain strong relationships with stakeholders across government, business, think tanks and academia Produce high-quality briefings, reports and presentations, and represent London & Partners at meetings, events and stakeholder forums. ‍ Key skills and experience ‍ Strong experience in economic growth, innovation or sector-focused policy roles within government, a thinktank, business organisation or related environment Proven ability to develop influential policy ideas, thought leadership and evidence-based recommendations Excellent project management skills, with experience leading complex programmes and coordinating multiple stakeholders Strong analytical and communication skills, with the ability to present complex information clearly for different audiences Experience building relationships with senior stakeholders across policy, government and industry. ‍ Application ‍ To apply, please share your CV using the link below before the closing deadline on Wednesday 27 th May 2026. ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Content and Digital Communications Manager
item.description: The Opportunity ‍ Lead the voice of a major UK digital infrastructure business, shaping high-impact content and social storytelling that drives awareness, engagement, and commercial growth. ‍ If you are energised by building brands through compelling storytelling, strategic content, and high-performing social channels, this role offers the opportunity to make a visible impact. This organisation is at the forefront of UK digital infrastructure, delivering connectivity solutions that support customers, landlords, and partners across the telecoms ecosystem. ‍ As Content & Digital Communications Lead, you will own and evolve the organisation’s content marketing and organic social strategy, helping shape how the business communicates across digital channels. You will play a central role in building a scalable always-on content engine that strengthens brand credibility, increases engagement, and supports measurable demand creation across both the core business and new growth propositions. ‍ This is a highly collaborative and fast-moving environment where creativity, strategic thinking, and strong execution are genuinely valued. You will work closely with senior stakeholders, support executive visibility, and create content that influences audiences across the industry. ‍ ‍ Main responsibilities ‍ Own the organisation’s LinkedIn and organic social strategy, driving corporate visibility, thought leadership, proposition education, and growth campaign engagement. Build and manage an always-on social publishing engine, including content series, campaign amplification, publishing cadence, and community engagement strategy. Create high-performing digital content optimised for LinkedIn and other social platforms, using strong storytelling, audience insight, and clear calls-to-action. Support CEO and senior leadership visibility through executive content drafting, voice adaptation, and strategic narrative alignment. Partner closely with Studio, Corporate Communications, and wider marketing teams to deliver integrated multi-channel campaigns and standout creative content. Monitor and optimise social and content performance, using insight and analytics to continuously improve engagement, audience growth, and downstream campaign impact. ‍ ‍ ‍ Key skills and experience required ‍ Deep expertise in LinkedIn, B2B digital content marketing, and organic social growth strategies. Strong understanding of content performance, audience engagement, and algorithm-informed publishing best practice. Exceptional copywriting and storytelling skills, with the ability to simplify complex topics into engaging and commercially impactful content. Experience building scalable content programmes, repeatable publishing rhythms, and executive thought leadership content. Proven success growing LinkedIn engagement and visibility for B2B brands and/or senior leadership teams.. Experience within telecoms, digital infrastructure, or closely related sectors is highly desirable. Ability to work collaboratively across marketing, communications, and senior stakeholder groups in a fast-paced environment. ‍ Application ‍ To apply, please do so below, or contact Jamie Ferguson at jamie.f@ellwoodatfield.com. ‍ ‍ ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Brand Marketing Manager
item.description: The Opportunity ‍ Drive the brand strategy of a leading UK digital infrastructure business, shaping high-impact campaigns that build market credibility, awareness, and commercial growth If you are excited by the opportunity to shape brand strategy, launch impactful campaigns, and position a business at the centre of a rapidly evolving industry, this role offers the chance to make a visible and lasting impact. This organisation operates at the forefront of UK digital infrastructure, delivering connectivity solutions that support customers, landlords, and partners across the telecoms ecosystem. ‍ As Brand Marketing Manager, you will lead the organisation’s brand marketing strategy and execution, building awareness, credibility, and market preference across priority audiences. You will create and deliver top and mid-funnel campaigns that support new products, vertical market expansion, and long-term brand growth across both the core business and new business acceleration initiatives. ‍ This is a highly collaborative role where you will work closely with Product, Sales, Performance Marketing, Content, and Creative teams to bring brand narratives to life across multiple channels. You will play a key role in establishing market positioning, strengthening category leadership, and building the foundation for measurable commercial growth. ‍ ‍ Main responsibilities ‍ Own the organisation’s brand marketing strategy, roadmap, and execution aligned to strategic priorities, core business growth, and new market expansion. Lead top and mid-funnel campaign strategy and delivery across new propositions, vertical markets, enterprise segments, and category positioning initiatives. Translate brand strategy and value propositions into compelling messaging frameworks, campaign territories, and evidence-led storytelling. Deliver integrated brand-led campaigns that drive awareness and consideration across digital, trade, paid, and earned channels. Own always-on brand activity including thought leadership, market education, and credibility-building campaigns. Partner closely with Product, Growth, Sales, Content, and Creative teams to ensure alignment between brand messaging and commercial priorities. Support campaign execution through clear briefing, stakeholder management, and collaboration with Brand & Creative Studio teams. Track and report on brand and upper-funnel performance including awareness, engagement, message cut-through, and share of voice. ‍ ‍ ‍ ‍ Key skills and experience required ‍ Strong expertise in B2B brand marketing and upper-funnel campaign strategy. Proven ability to build awareness and consideration campaigns for complex products, propositions, or technical services. Strong narrative development, positioning, and messaging capability. Experience launching new products, propositions, or vertical market campaigns. Understanding of how brand and upper-funnel activity supports down streamdemand generation and lead conversion. Experience within telecoms, digital infrastructure, or closely related sectors is highly desirable. Strong stakeholder management, prioritisation, and campaign delivery skills within fast-paced environments. ‍ ‍ ‍ Application ‍ To apply, please do so below, or contact Jamie Ferguson at jamie.f@ellwoodatfield.com.
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Director of Communications
item.description: Ellwood Atfield are proud to be partnering with a leading public sector regulatory body that plays a vital role in upholding information rights and data protection standards across the UK. ‍ ‍ The Opportunity ‍ This is a rare opportunity for a senior communications leader to join an organisation at a pivotal moment and shape the way it communicates its purpose, decisions and regulatory authority to a wide range of stakeholders. ‍ Reporting to the Executive Director of Communications and Public Affairs and as a member of the Senior Leadership Team, the Director of Communications will set the vision and strategic direction for a modern, high-performing communications function that is embedded early in organisational decision-making and will be leading a multidisciplinary team supported by two direct reports and oversee a team of30. ‍ ‍ Key Responsibilities ‍ Set and lead the communications strategy, aligned to organisational priorities and regulatory objectives, working in close partnership with the Executive Director. Position communications as a core strategic function, embedded early in planning, policy and regulatory decision making.. Provide senior counsel to the Executive Team on sensitive, high-profile and reputationally significant issues, leading the organisation’s approach to reputation management and crisis readiness. Champion an insight-driven, outcomes-focused approach to communications, using research, data and evaluation to inform strategy and measure impact. Lead a digital first culture, ensuring channels, content and formats reflect how audiences consume information, while encouraging creativity and innovation. Own and shape the organisation’s overarching narrative and lead proactive engagement to build public trust and confidence. Work with internal communications to align internal and external messaging, supporting leadership communication capability and consistency across the organisation. Lead, inspire and develop a collaborative, high-performing team of around 30 people, fostering a culture of confidence, clarity, accountability and continuous improvement. ‍ ‍ Experience Required ‍ Senior leadership experience running a substantial communications function in a high profile, scrutinised environment. Proven experience advising senior leaders on sensitive, complex and reputationally significant communications issues. A demonstrable record of using communications to shape outcomes Experience setting and delivering communications strategy across multiple disciplines, including media, digital, campaigns, stakeholder engagement and events. Strong track record of leading and developing senior managers and building high-performing, confident teams. Experience managing resources and budgets, including commissioning and working with agencies to enhance capacity and capability. Deep understanding of insight-driven, audience-led communications, including digital first approaches and modern communications practice. ‍ ‍ How to apply ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunters for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Chief Executive Officer
item.description: The opportunity: ‍ The British Compressed Gases Association (BCGA) is the leading UK trade association representing the interests of 100+ member companies. These companies, in the industrial, food and medical gases industries, include manufacturers and suppliers of liquefied, dissolved and compressed gases as well as the associated gaseous pressure systems, pipework, components and related equipment. ‍ Members also provide specialist safety, health, environment, quality, inspection and training services. Today, BCGA members employ over 19,000 people directly and account for an annual turnover of over £2.7 bn. ‍ The new Chief Executive Officer will lead BCGA and deliver its mission as the UK’s authoritative body for safety, standards and best practice across the industrial and medical gases sector. ‍ ‍ Responsibilities include: ‍ Setting and deliver BCGA’s strategic direction, ensuring the Association remains relevant, influential and responsive to sector priorities. Leading effective operational delivery, financial stewardship and robust governance in partnership with the BCGA Council. Safeguarding and strengthening BCGA’s reputation as a trusted technical authority on safety, standards and best practice. Representing BCGA and the wider compressed gases sector with credibility, impartiality and integrity to members, regulators, government and industry stakeholders. ‍ Requirements include: ‍ Senior leadership experience, ideally gained in a complex, technical, regulated or membership-based organisation. Experience of operating successfully in environments where safety, standards, governance and stakeholder confidence are critical. Experience engaging with government, regulators, industry bodies or professional institutions. Awareness of the challenges and opportunities facing technical, regulated and safety-critical sectors. ‍ ‍ This exciting opportunity is open to candidates with the credibility and judgement to step into a high-profile leadership role, whether as an experienced CEO or a senior leader ready to take on a first CEO appointment. ‍ How to apply: ‍ For more information, please consult the detailed candidate pack and apply with a supporting statement and CV via the form below. ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Senior Policy Officer
item.description: The Organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with the Royal College of Surgeons of England on the appointment of two Senior Policy Officers. ‍ The Royal College of Surgeons of England is entering an exciting new phase in its policy and influencing work, with the organisation evolving its Policy and Campaigns function to deliver greater external impact across the issues affecting surgeons and patients. ‍ Representing over 30,000 surgeons and dental surgeons worldwide, the College plays a leading role in shaping standards in surgical care, supporting the profession and influencing debate across major healthcare and workforce challenges. ‍ ‍ The Opportunity ‍ This is an excellent opportunity for a policy professional who enjoys combining analytical thinking with practical, outcomes-focused policy development. ‍ As Senior Policy Officer, you will support the delivery of the College’s policy objectives across key issues affecting surgical practice, workforce and healthcare delivery. Working closely with clinicians and colleagues across policy, campaigns, publishing, and communications, you will help develop evidence-based policy positions designed to influence government, regulators and NHS bodies. ‍ You will also play an important role in horizon scanning, identifying emerging political, healthcare and regulatory developments, and ensuring the College remains a credible and influential voice on issues impacting surgery and patient care. ‍ ‍ Key Responsibilities ‍ Develop evidence-led policy positions across issues impacting surgical practice, workforce and standards. Produce high-quality policy briefings, consultation responses and stakeholder briefings and engagement materials. Monitor political, NHS and regulatory developments to identify emerging risks and influencing opportunities. Work closely with campaigns, communications and public affairs colleagues to support wider influencing objectives. Build strong relationships with clinicians, healthcare organisations, regulators and government stakeholders. Support senior stakeholders and College spokespeople with policy advice and external engagement. ‍ ‍ Person specification ‍ Develop Experience working within policy, public affairs or influencing roles in healthcare, government, regulation or membership organisations. Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate complex information into practical policy recommendations. Excellent written communication skills and experience producing materials for senior audiences. Understanding of political, healthcare or regulatory environments. Strong stakeholder engagement skills and ability to work collaboratively across teams. Comfortable working at pace and managing competing objectives. ‍ ‍ How to apply: ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. Closing date: 9:00am Friday 12th June Interview date: Week commencing 22nd June We will only be able to consider applications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. ‍ ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‍ Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‍

item.title: Policy Officer
item.description: The Organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with the Royal College of Surgeons of England on the appointment a Policy Officers. ‍ The Royal College of Surgeons of England is entering an exciting new phase in its policy and influencing work, with the organisation evolving its Policy and Campaigns function to deliver greater external impact across the issues affecting surgeons and patients. ‍ Representing over 30,000 surgeons and dental surgeons worldwide, the College plays a leading role in shaping standards in surgical care, supporting the profession and influencing debate across major healthcare and workforce challenges. ‍ ‍ The Opportunity ‍ This is an excellent opportunity for someone looking to build a career in health policy and influencing while helping shape the future direction of surgical policy and influencing work at one of the UK’s most respected professional bodies. ‍ As Policy Officer, you will support the development and delivery of policy activity across a wide range of issues affecting surgical practice, workforce and healthcare delivery. Working closely with clinicians and colleagues across policy, campaigns, publications, and communications, you will monitor and analyse political, NHS and regulatory developments, identify emerging trends and support the development of accessible policy insights and member communications. ‍ The successful candidate will join a collaborative and ambitious team environment with the opportunity to help shape the development of surgical policy, campaigns and member engagement work at one of the UK’s leading professional membership organisations. ‍ ‍ Key Responsibilities ‍ Monitor political, NHS and regulatory developments relevant to surgery and dental surgery, identifying emerging issues and opportunities. Produce high-quality concise policy updates, briefings and analysis for colleagues, members and stakeholders. Support the development of consultation responses, policy papers and evidence-based recommendations. Undertake research and horizon scanning across healthcare policy, workforce, training and patient safety issues. Draft clear, accessible communications to members, explaining policy developments and their impact. Work collaboratively with colleagues across campaigns, communications and engagement to support wider influencing objectives. ‍ ‍ Person specification ‍ Experience monitoring policy or political developments and summarising key implications. Strong research and analytical skills, with the ability to synthesise information from multiple sources. Excellent written communication skills, including producing clear briefings and policy updates. Understanding of UK policy processes in healthcare or regulatory environments. Strong organisational skills, attention to detail, and ability to work collaboratively at pace. ‍ ‍ How to apply: ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. Closing date: 9:00am Friday 12th June Interview date: Week commencing 22nd June We will only be able to consider applications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. ‍ ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. item.title: Engagement Lead (Higher Education)
item.description: About the organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield is delighted to have partnered with a respected trade association who are looking for an Engagement Lead on a permanent basis. The organisation is renowned for working alongside Government and business leaders to provide insights and advice around securing investment opportunities in Asia. ‍ The organisation work closely with its members to help businesses influence policy, connect with decision-makers and identify opportunities for growth. ‍ The opportunity ‍ A key element to the organisation’s growth strategy lies within identifying and leveraging opportunities within the Higher Education sector. As an Engagement Lead, you will play a crucial part in driving expansion; operating as a visible leader for UK university clients. You will optimise brand presence throughout the sector, through partnerships, policy influence and market knowledge. You will also manage a broad range of relationships, engaging with policy teams relating to international student recruitment, cross University partnerships as well as market entry for UK universities. ‍ Key Responsibilities ‍ Establish and maintain client relationships across the higher education sector through proactive engagement. Liaise with clients to understand their issues and strategic needs pertaining to cross country University relations. Promote awareness of launchpad and other services provided by the trade association within the higher education sector. Optimise driving client retention and acquisition through innovative recruitment strategies and market insights. ‍ ‍ Key Skills and Experience Required ‍ Extensive experience in UK Higher Education (preferably in international offices) student recruitment, or partnerships. Strong understanding of sector challenges and solutions for growth in fast-evolving landscapes. Proven ability to quickly build trust withsenior external stakeholders including University Vice-Chancellors and Deans. Able to manage various aspects of stakeholder and client relationships including lead generation, pitching, closure and delivery oversight. Good understanding of HE decision-making as well as student flows and visa compliance obstacles. Adept in conducting qualitative/quantitative research to inform policy reports/briefings on HE trends, and sector reforms. Confident representing the organisation at external events. ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Integrated Communications Manager
item.description: Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with a globally respected membership body at the forefront of maritime innovation to recruit anIntegrated Communications Manager. ‍ For more than 160 years, the Royal Institute of Naval Architects (RINA) has advanced the art and science of naval architecture through technical excellence, thought leadership and global collaboration. Today, it brings together professionals across more than 140 countries working across everything from green propulsion and AI to warship resilience and autonomy. ‍ This is a newly created role at the heart of a fast-moving marketing and communications team during a significant period of organisational transformation and growth. ‍ The Integrated Communications Manager will lead integrated email marketing, CRM-led communications and cross-organisational campaign planning -helping grow membership, increase readership of The Naval Architect , expand specialist newsletter audiences and support wider business development activity. ‍ This is not a traditional hard-sell marketing role. RINA’s approach is “helpful first, selling second”: using high-quality content, audience insight and carefully targeted communications to build trust, engagement and long-term member value. ‍ The successful candidate will combine strong copywriting and campaign instincts with hands-on experience across CRM and email marketing. Maritime experience is not required, but curiosity, commercial instinct and an ability to engage credibly with technical subject matter are essential. ‍ ‍ Key responsibilities include: ‍ ‍ Leading integrated email marketing andcommunications campaigns across membership, publications and businessdevelopment Managing CRM segmentation, automation andaudience targeting Creating and overseeing an organisation-wideemail communications calendar Supporting cross-sell and upsell campaigns andconversion journeys Working closely with content, events andpublications colleagues to maximise audience engagement Tracking campaign performance and usinginsight to improve effectiveness and conversion ‍ ‍ We are particularly interested in speaking with candidates fromintegrated communications, CRM, membership marketing, publishing, subscriptionor B2B content-led environments. ‍ RINA offers a highly flexible and collaborative working culture,with significant scope for creativity, experimentation and visible impact. ‍ Closing date: Friday 12 June Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, so earlyapplications are encouraged. We will only be able to considerapplications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. ‍ ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs,communications, policy, and advocacy. item.title: Industry Relations Manager
item.description: About the organisation: ‍ Ellwood Atfield is thrilled to be supporting a nationally recognised organisation at the forefront of one of the UK’s largest infrastructure and consumer engagement programmes. The Opportunity: ‍ This is a highly collaborative and externally focused role within the Corporate Affairs function, offering the opportunity to work closely with senior stakeholders across industry, regulation and partner organisations. ‍ The Industry Relations Manager will lead engagement activity with key industry partners, providing insight, coordination and strategic support across a range of programmes and initiatives. You will monitor sector developments, identify implications for stakeholders and support the delivery of collaborative projects that contribute to long-term organisational objectives. ‍ This is an exciting opportunity for someone who enjoys building relationships, navigating complex stakeholder environments, and translating technical or policy information into clear, actionable insight. Main responsibilities: ‍ Build and maintain strong relationships with industry stakeholders, acting as a trusted point of contact. Support the development and delivery of stakeholder engagement strategies and collaborative projects. Monitor sector, regulatory and policy developments, identifying potential risks and opportunities. Produce clear briefings, presentations and insight to support internal decision-making. Work cross-functionally with communications, insights and corporate affairs teams to align stakeholder activity. Represent the organisation at external meetings, forums and industry events. Support and develop junior colleagues, helping ensure high-quality delivery across the function. ‍ ‍ Key skills and experience required: ‍ Experience delivering stakeholder engagement or industry relations activity within a complex environment. Strong relationship management skills, with the ability to influence and engage senior stakeholders. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including presenting complex information clearly. Strong organisational and project management skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities. Ability to interpret policy, regulatory or operational developments and communicate implications effectively. Experience working within a regulated sector or complex stakeholder environment would be advantageous. ‍ ‍ How to apply: ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. The deadline for applications is Tuesday2 nd June 2026, however we may close the advert earlier if we receive sufficient applications, so we would encourage you to apply sooner rather than later. ‍ EllwoodAtfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications,policy, and advocacy. item.title: Head of External Affairs
item.description: ‍ The organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with The Health Foundation on the appointment of a Head of External Affairs. ‍ The Health Foundation is an independent charitable organisation committed to building a healthier UK. Through research, analysis, convening and influencing, the Foundation plays a leading role in shaping debate and driving practical change across health and care. ‍ At a time of significant challenge and change across the health system, the Foundation is continuing to strengthen its voice, profile and influence across Westminster, Whitehall, the media and the wider health policy landscape. ‍ ‍ The opportunity ‍ This is a high-profile opportunity to lead external affairs, media relations and stakeholder engagement for one of the UK’s most respected and influential health policy organisations. ‍ Reporting into the Deputy Director of Communications, the Head of External Affairs will lead a busy and high-performing team responsible for media, public affairs and stakeholder relations activity across the Foundation’s broad portfolio of work. ‍ The successful candidate will play a central role in shaping and delivering the Foundation’s influencing strategy - identifying opportunities to position the organisation at the forefront of political, policy and public debate across health and care. ‍ Working closely with senior leadership, the role will provide strategic counsel across media, government relations, reputational issues and stakeholder engagement, while helping ensure the Foundation’s work reaches and influences key audiences nationally. ‍ This role would suit an experienced communications, public affairs or external affairs leader who combines strong political and policy awareness with excellent judgement, leadership capability and highly developed stakeholder management skills. ‍ Key responsibilities ‍ Lead the Foundation’s external affairs, media relations and influencing activity across health and public policy issues. Develop high-profile and timely opportunities to position the Foundation at the centre of policy, political and media debate. Oversee engagement with senior stakeholders across Westminster, Whitehall, media and the wider health sector. Lead and develop the Foundation’s media relations strategy, ensuring strong profile and impactful coverage across target audiences. Provide strategic communications and public affairs advice to senior leaders and colleagues across the organisation. Develop compelling messaging, briefings and external affairs materials for a range of audiences. Lead, motivate and develop ahigh-performing external affairs team. Contribute to the wider leadership of the Communications function as a senior member of the management team. ‍ ‍ Person specification ‍ Significant experience leading external affairs, media, communications or public affairs functions within complex organisations. Strong understanding of UK politics, public policy and influencing environments. Demonstrable track record of delivering profile, impact and organisational influence through integrated external affairs strategies. Excellent stakeholder management skills with the ability to build credibility at senior levels. Highly accomplished written and verbal communication skills. Experience leading and developing teams within fast-paced and high-profile environments. Ability to manage complex issues with sound judgement, discretion and political awareness. Interest in health policy, public policy and mission-led work. ‍ ‍ How to apply ‍ For more information or if you would like an informal discussion about the roles, please contact Felicity or Josh on: felicity@ellwoodatfield.com / joshua@ellwoodatfield.com ‍ To access the job description, please click here. ‍ To apply, please submit your CV and a supporting statement outlining how you meet the essential criteria for the role via the portal. ‍ Closing date: Wednesday 17th June 2026 ‍ We will only be able to consider applications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. item.title: Deputy Director of Communications
item.description: ‍ ‍ The organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with the Health Foundation on the appointment of a Deputy Director of Communications. ‍ The Health Foundation is an independent charitable organisation committed to building a healthier UK. Through research, analysis, convening and influencing, the Foundation plays a leading role in shaping debate and driving practical change across health and care. ‍ At a time of significant challenge and change across the health system, the Foundation is continuing to strengthen its voice, profile and influence across Westminster, Whitehall, the media and the wider health policy landscape. ‍ ‍ The opportunity ‍ This is a significant senior leadership opportunity within one of the UK’s most respectedand influential health policy organisations. ‍ Reporting to the Director of Communications, the Deputy Director of Communications will play a central role in shaping and delivering integrated communications strategies across the Foundation’s major thematic programmes of work. ‍ The successful candidate will work closely with senior leadership across the organisation to ensure communications activity delivers impact, strengthens organisational influence and supports the Foundation’s ambition to be one of the leading voices in UK public policy. ‍ The remit is broad and highly strategic - spanning communications strategy, stakeholder engagement, external affairs, reputation, thought leadership and executive counsel. The role also sits on the Foundation’s Senior Leadership Team and plays an important role in the wider leadership of the Communications directorate. ‍ This opportunity would suit an experienced strategic communications leader with strong policy instincts, excellent leadership capability and experience operating within complex, high-profile or mission-led organisations. ‍ ‍ Key responsibilities ‍ Lead integrated communications strategies across major thematic programmes of work. Develop clear, compelling and influential messaging across a broad range of policy and organisational priorities. Work closely with senior colleagues to identify influencing, convening and stakeholder engagement opportunities. Provide strategic communications advice to the Chief Executive, directors and senior colleagues across the Foundation. Oversee communications planning and delivery across a range of high-profile projects and programmes. Represent the Foundation externally with media, political and stakeholder audiences. Lead and develop multidisciplinary communications teams. Contribute to the wider leadership and strategic direction of the Communications function and the Foundation more broadly. ‍ ‍ Person specification ‍ Significant experience leading strategic communications functions within complex organisations. Strong understanding of UK politics, public policy and influencing environments. Demonstrable track record of developing integrated communications strategies that deliver organisational impact and influence. Excellent stakeholder engagement and relationship-building skills. Strong written and verbal communication capability, including experience handling complex and sensitive issues. Experience advising senior leaders and operating at executive level. Leadership experience within fast-paced, high-profile or mission-led environments. Interest in health policy, health care and improving public outcomes. ‍ ‍ ‍ How to apply ‍ For more information or if you would like an informal discussion about the roles, please contact Felicity or Josh on: felicity@ellwoodatfield.com / joshua@ellwoodatfield.com ‍ To access the job description, please click here. ‍ To apply, please submit your CV and a supporting statement outlining how you meet the essential criteria for the role via the portal. ‍ Closing date: Wednesday 17th June 2026 ‍ We will only be able to consider applications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email.
