item.title: Senior Campaigns Officer
item.description: ‍ The organisation Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with the Royal College of Surgeons of England on the appointment of two Senior Campaigns Officers. ‍ The Royal College of Surgeons of England is one of the world’s leading professional surgical membership organisations, advancing surgical standards and improving patient care across the UK and internationally. ‍ The College is entering an exciting new chapter, launching a new corporate strategy alongside newly structured Policy and Campaigns functions designed to deliver more focused, impactful and outcomes-driven influencing activity. ‍ ‍ The opportunity ‍ This is an exciting opportunity to help deliver high-impact campaigns focused on improving surgical care, influencing health policy, and driving meaningful change for surgeons and patients. ‍ Working closely with the Campaigns Manager and colleagues across policy, communications and public affairs, the Senior Campaigns Officer will support the delivery of integrated campaigns designed to influence governments, NHS bodies, regulators and wider healthcare stakeholders. ‍ The role will suit someone with strong political awareness, excellent communication skills and the ability to translate complex policy issues into compelling campaign activity. You will thrive in a fast-paced environment, spotting opportunities to influence debate and helping deliver campaigns that achieve real-world impact. ‍ Please also click here to see the applicant pack and introductory letter from Patrick Leahy, Director of Communications, outlining the College’s wider vision for the new Policy and Campaigns function and the opportunity these roles present. ‍ ‍ Responsibilities ‍ Support the planning and delivery of high-impact campaigns aligned to the College’s influencing priorities. Translate policy, evidence and insight into compelling campaign messaging and calls to action. Work collaboratively with policy, communications and public affairs colleagues to deliver integrated influencing activity. Support member engagement and mobilisation activity where appropriate. Monitor political, policy and media developments, identifying opportunities to advance the College’s position. Build productive relationships with external stakeholders and partner organisations. Use insight, evaluation and campaign performance data to support continuous improvement. ‍ ‍ Person specification ‍ Experience delivering campaigns, advocacy or influencing activity within policy, public affairs, healthcare, charities, membership bodies or related environments. Strong understanding of the UK political and health policy landscape. Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to develop persuasive messaging for different audiences. Ability to translate complex policy or evidence into clear and engaging campaign activity. Strong organisational and project management skills with the ability to manage competing priorities. Collaborative and confident working style with strong stakeholder engagement skills. Experience of digital campaigning tools or techniques would be beneficial. ‍ ‍ How to apply ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. ‍ Closing date: Friday 19th June ‍ We will only be able to consider applications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. ‍ ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. item.title: Campaigns Manager
item.description: ‍ The organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with the Royal College of Surgeons of England on the appointment of a Campaigns Manager. ‍ The Royal College of Surgeons of England is one of the world’s leading professional surgical membership organisations, advancing surgical standards and improving patient care across the UK and internationally. ‍ The College is entering an exciting new chapter, launching a new corporate strategy alongside newly structured Policy and Campaigns functions designed to deliver more focused, impactful and outcomes-driven influencing activity. ‍ ‍ The opportunity ‍ This is an exciting opportunity to help shape and deliver high-impact campaigns focused on improving surgical care, influencing health policy, and driving meaningful change for surgeons and patients. ‍ Working closely with the Head of Press and Campaigns, the Campaigns Manager will lead a small number of focused, strategic campaigns designed to influence governments, NHS bodies, regulators and wider healthcare stakeholders. The role will suit someone who combines strong political and strategic instincts with creativity, pace and excellent stakeholder management skills. ‍ You will play a key role in embedding a more modern, delivery-focused campaigning function, working collaboratively across policy, communications and public affairs to deliver campaigns that cut through and achieve real-world impact. ‍ Please also click here to see the applicant pack and introductory letter from Patrick Leahy, Director of Communications, outlining the College’s wider vision for the new Policy and Campaigns function and the opportunity these roles present [LINK HERE] ‍ ‍ Responsibilities ‍ Lead the development and delivery of high-impact campaigns aligned to the College’s influencing priorities. Translate policy, evidence and insight into compelling narratives and clear calls to action. Work closely with policy, communications and public affairs colleagues to deliver integrated influencing activity. Identify and respond quickly to political, media and stakeholder opportunities. Build productive relationships with external partners, stakeholders and healthcare organisations. Oversee member engagement and mobilisation activity where appropriate. Line manage and support the Senior Campaigns Officer. Use insight, evaluation and campaign performance data to refine activity and demonstrate impact. ‍ ‍ Person specification ‍ Significant experience delivering campaigns within policy, public affairs, healthcare, membership bodies, charities or related environments. Strong understanding of the UK political and health policy landscape. . Experience influencing decision-makers and managing stakeholder relationships. Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to develop persuasive campaign messaging. Strong project management skills with the ability to manage competing priorities at pace. Collaborative and confident working style with the ability to engage senior stakeholders and clinicians. Experience managing or supporting junior colleagues is desirable. ‍ ‍ ‍ How to apply ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. ‍ Closing date: Friday 19th June 2026 ‍ We will only be able to consider applications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. ‍ ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. item.title: Regulatory Affairs Lead
item.description: ‍ The organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield are proud to partner with Exchange for Change, the not-for-profit organisation appointed by the UK government to design, build and operate the UK's new Deposit Return Scheme (DRS) for single-use drinks containers made from PET plastic, steel and aluminium. ‍ This scheme which launches on 1st October 2027 across England, Scotland, Northern Ireland will be the world’s biggest DRS and one of the most ambitious environmental infrastructure programmes in the UK’s history. ‍ ‍ The opportunity ‍ Exchange for Change is seeking a Regulatory Affairs Lead to take ownership of the organisation's regulatory strategy, ensuring full compliance with DRS legislation and all statutory obligations. The role will be responsible for developing and executing a robust regulatory plan, while proactively monitoring the external environment to anticipate change and futureproof both Exchange for Change and the DRS. ‍ ‍ Central to the position is the ability to build and maintain strong, collaborative relationships with both with regulators and internal stakeholders, to drive engagement, confidence and transparency. The Regulatory Affairs Lead will play a key role in upholding the highest standards of governance and accountability, ensuring Exchange for Change operates with integrity at every level as it delivers one of the most significant environmental programmes in UK history. ‍ ‍ Key Responsibilities ‍ Develop and implement Exchange for Change's regulatory strategy, ensuring full compliance with UK DRS legislation while proactively monitoring the policy landscape to identify, assess and mitigate risks, and futureproof the organisation and the scheme. Lead Exchange for Change's input into external policy and regulatory consultations, representing the organisation at key stakeholder forums and shaping regulatory activity in line with strategic priorities. Act as the strategic lead for all regulator relationships, convening and directing cross-functional teams to deliver tailored engagement strategies across each nation, ensuring consistent messaging, coordinated approaches and effective intelligence-sharing. Develop and implement a proactive regulatory affairs engagement strategy, collaborating closely with the Head of External Affairs to align and streamline engagement with both regulators and government representatives. Oversee all regulatory reporting and compliance submissions, including quarterly compliance reports, annual funding reports and operational plans for England, Northern Ireland and Scotland. Recruit and line manage a Regulatory Affairs Manager, fostering a culture of collaboration, high performance and shared accountability across the regulatory affairs function. ‍ ‍ ‍ Experience required ‍ Extensive senior level experience in leading regulatory affairs, compliance or public affairs within a multi-stakeholder and matrixed organisation including FMCG, utilities, transport, logistics and environmental management. Strong experience in working with government departments, regulators and trade associations. Proven capability engaging diverse stakeholders across industry, supply chains, regulators and government. Strong understanding of devolved regulatory systems and differing policy/legislative contexts in England, Scotland and Northern Ireland. Strategic thinker who can assess, manage and mitigate sensitive issues/risks, balancing political, operational and commercial considerations effectively. Exceptional communication skills, capable of translating complex operational issues for political, industry and internal audiences. Understanding of UK environmental and waste management regulation would be beneficial. ‍ ‍ ‍ Benefits ‍ 34 days holiday (Including bank holidays) Car salary sacrifice scheme ‍ ‍ How to apply: ‍ To apply, please submit the form below by Monday 15th June, 2026 and include a copy of your CV. ‍ ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunters for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‍ Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. If you have not done so already, please let us know if you require any support so we can make the right adjustments and considerations should they be required. ‍ ‍

item.title: Communications Executive
item.description: ‍ About the organisation: ‍ Ellwood Atfield are proud to be supporting a leading transport and logistics company to recruit Communications Executive. The opportunity: ‍ Working as part of a collaborative communications team, you will support the planning, creation and delivery of content across a variety of channels. You will help ensure communications are clear, consistent and aligned with organisational priorities while contributing to initiatives that strengthen reputation and employee engagement. ‍ The successful candidate will be a strong communicator with excellent writing skills and the ability to tailor your approach for different audiences. You'll be organised, proactive and comfortable managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. ‍ Regular travel across the UK will be required to engage with colleagues, gather content and support communications activity across multiple locations. ‍ Main responsibilities: ‍ Create and deliver engaging content across internal and external communications channels, including digital, social media and corporate platforms. Support the planning, coordination and delivery of communications campaigns and activities aligned to organisational priorities. Build relationships with colleagues across the business, travelling regularly across the UK to gather content, develop stories and support stakeholder engagement. Monitor media coverage and communications performance, providing insight and reporting to support informed decision-making. Collaborate with internal stakeholders and external partners to ensure consistent messaging and the timely delivery of communications materials, briefings and updates. ‍ ‍ ‍ Key skills and experience required: ‍ Experience in communications, public relations, journalism, marketing or a related field. Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Experience creating content for digital and social media channels. Strong organisational skills and attention to detail. Professional judgement and discretion when handling sensitive information. Willingness and ability to travel regularly across the UK. ‍ How to apply: ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. ‍ Application deadline: 9am on 17 th June ‍ ‍ ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. item.title: Senior Government Relations Manager
item.description: ‍ The Opportunity ‍ ‍ Our client, a leading global technology company, seeks a Senior Government Relations Manager to join its small and highly effective UK public affairs team. You will work alongside senior leaders in the business to analyse and understand Government policies and maintain the organisation’s great reputation with politicians and other senior stakeholders. This role will support business objectives by helping to position the company as a trusted advisor to Government and policy-makers across a broad range of issues. ‍ ‍ Main Responsibilities ‍ Develop and execute the Government Relations programme, engaging with key political and industry influencers. Analyse Government policies and their potential impacts, providing constructive alternatives. Build and maintain relationships with Government, trade associations, and other senior stakeholders. Provide clear and concise briefings on policy developments and their implications. Coordinate internal communication on political monitoring and reporting. ‍ Key Skills and Experience ‍ In-depth understanding of the UK political process and policy development. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to present complex ideas clearly. Experience in managing Government Affairs programmes in a large commercial organisation or public affairs agency. Strong relationship-building skills and ability to navigate multicultural environments. Experience of working in technology, telecoms or digital policy is highly desirable. ‍ Application ‍ This is a full-time position and we are particularly interested in hearing if you are immediately available or have a short notice period. Please apply by submitting your CV via the link below. ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Director of Campaigns
item.description: We are seeking an exceptional communicator to join our client’s Executive Leadership Team and lead the development of a bold, influential, and campaigns-led approach to advocacy, policy, and communications. ‍ This is a newly created leadership role with the opportunity to redefine how our client engages with Government, policy makers, the media, members, and key stakeholders. As Director of Campaigns, you will be responsible for ensuring the organisation is recognised as the authoritative and unified voice of the sector, driving meaningful policy outcomes and raising awareness of the industry's critical contribution to the UK economy. ‍ You will lead the integration of policy, public affairs, communications, and stakeholder engagement to deliver high-impact campaigns that influence decision-makers, strengthen reputation, and create lasting value for members. ‍ ‍ The Opportunity ‍ ‍ As a member of the Executive Leadership Team, you will shape organisational strategy and drive a "campaigns first" culture across all advocacy activity. ‍ Working closely with colleagues you will create and deliver compelling campaigns that mobilise support, influence policy, and position the organisation at the centre of key national debates. ‍ This is an outstanding opportunity for an experienced campaigns, public affairs, or policy leader looking to make a significant impact within one of the UK's most important sectors. ‍ ‍ Key Responsibilities include: ‍ Develop and deliver a strategic, insight-led campaigns programme that aligns with organisational priorities, drives member value, and delivers measurable policy and advocacy outcomes. Lead the development of evidence-based policy positions and thought leadership, translating complex issues into compelling narratives that influence political, regulatory, media, and industry audiences. Build and maintain influential relationships with Government, parliamentarians, regulators, policymakers, members, and industry stakeholders to strengthen the organisation's voice and impact. Enhance the organisation's external profile through strategic communications, media engagement, impactful storytelling, and acting as a senior spokesperson when required. Foster collaboration across membership, policy, communications, and external partners to maximise campaign engagement, amplify key messages, and strengthen sector-wide advocacy. Lead, develop, and inspire a high-performing team, ensuring effective resource management, accountability, innovation, and delivery against strategic objectives. ‍ ‍ About You ‍ We are looking for a strategic and politically astute leader with a proven ability to deliver influential campaigns and shape public debate. ‍ Requirements include: ‍ Proven experience leading high-profile advocacy, public affairs, or stakeholder engagement campaigns, ideally within a trade association, membership organisation, professional body, or similarly complex stakeholder environment. Deep understanding of Government, Parliament, public policy, and political decision-making processes, with the ability to influence agendas and secure strategic outcomes. Exceptional communication, stakeholder engagement, and narrative-building skills, with the ability to translate complex issues into compelling and persuasive messaging. Strong media and external affairs experience, including acting as a credible spokesperson and building influential relationships across Government, media, regulatory, and industry networks. Demonstrable success in leading, developing, and inspiring high-performing teams within a fast-paced, results-driven environment. Commercially astute and strategically minded, with the credibility, judgement, and leadership capability to operate effectively at Executive Leadership Team level and build consensus across diverse stakeholder groups. ‍ ‍ This is a rare opportunity to build and lead a newly created function with significant strategic influence. You will help shape the future of an industry that underpins the UK's economy, leading campaigns that drive real change and ensure the sector's voice is heard where it matters most. ‍ If you are an ambitious and influential leader with a passion for advocacy, policy, and strategic communications, we would love to hear from you. ‍ ‍ How to apply: ‍ For more information and to apply, please share a copy of your CV via the form below. ‍ Deadline for applications is midday on Thursday 2nd July. ‍
item.dc:creator: Ellwood Atfield

item.title: Digital Marketing Specialist
item.description: About the organisation: ‍ Ellwood Atfield is proud to be partnering with a leading membership body within the health and social sciences sector. ‍ The opportunity: ‍ As Digital Marketing Specialist, you will play a key role in delivering and optimising digital marketing activity across paid media and email marketing. This is a technical marketing role which combines PPC, paid social, email campaigns and marketing automation, with responsibility for driving brand awareness, audience engagement and conversion across a range of products and services. You will manage Google Ads activity, including Google Ad Grants, oversee paid social campaigns across multiple platforms, and work closely with agency partners to ensure strong performance and return on investment. You will use data and insight to inform decisions, track campaign performance, and report on results to identify opportunities for continuous improvement. You will contribute to email marketing campaigns, customer journeys and content creation, working collaboratively with colleagues across the organisation. This is an excellent opportunity for a digitally savvy marketer to make a visible impact within a purpose-driven organisation while helping to shape and develop its digital marketing capabilities. ‍ Main responsibilities: ‍ Plan, deliver and optimise PPC and paid social campaigns across Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn and TikTok. Conduct audience targeting, keyword research, A/B testing and campaign optimisation to improve engagement and conversion. Set up tracking and reporting using GA4 and other analytics tools, providing actionable insights and recommendations. Create and deliver email marketing campaigns, automated customer journeys and compelling digital content, while collaborating with stakeholders on wider marketing initiatives. Collaborate with internal teams and external partners to support integrated marketing campaigns and drive audience engagement. Key skills and experience required: ‍ Proven experience managing PPC and paid social campaigns across platforms including Google Ads, Meta, LinkedIn and TikTok. Strong knowledge of digital marketing analytics, conversion tracking, campaign reporting and performance optimisation. Experience delivering email marketing campaigns, automation journeys and audience segmentation strategies. Solid understanding of SEO, CRO, digital best practice, GDPR compliance and emerging marketing technologies. Highly analytical and commercially minded, with excellent communication, copywriting and stakeholder management skills. How to apply: ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. ‍ The deadline for applications is Monday 26th June 2026, however we may close the advert earlier if we receive sufficient applications, so we would encourage you to apply sooner rather than later. ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy, and advocacy. An employee-owned boutique search firm, we help you hire the most senior leaders and everyone who reports to them. We promise to connect talented people, smoothly and efficiently. ‍ Our purpose is to create important and enduring relationships. We are an equal opportunities employer and promote diversity across our profession to ensure every individual has the opportunity to perform at their best. item.title: Policy Manager (Prudential Reporting and Tax)
item.description: The organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield is proud to be partnering with a leading trade association which represents the interests of members across a broad spectrum of financial services. Working closely with industry leaders, government, regulators and the media, the organisation champions the developments of a thriving sector which serves society and ensuring that the UK retains its role as a global leader in financial services. ‍ The opportunity ‍ The trade association are looking for a Policy Manager to support the Prudential Reporting and Tax team in its engagement with members and external stakeholders across government, banks and regulators to lead on various area of policy development relating to prudential reporting and tax rules. ‍ ‍ The role will be involved in undertaking evidence gathering and content development contributing to reports, thought leadership and cross-cutting initiatives such as media content, blogs and online material. This will include items such as the Total Tax Contribution document, Plan for Growth documents and other reports. The role will also offer opportunities to deputise for principles where needed and run individual workstreams as required as well as briefing and drafting of related papers, media content, blogs and online material. Key Responsibilities ‍ Own relationships with subject matter experts and members on subjects relating to prudential reporting and tax. Supporting development of written responses to consultations discussion papers issued by the Prudential Regulation Authority, the Bank of England, HM Treasury, HMRC, the Department of Business and Trade and other external stakeholders. Ensuring policy proposals are supported by credible and integrated research. Working closely with media and communication teams to raise awareness of policy positions and priorities. Key skills ‍ Ability to respond to member needs whilst actively seeking opportunities to innovate and add value. A strong working knowledge of the UK banking landscape including knowledge of the challenges and opportunities impacting the future of the UK banking industry. A track record of providing strategic analysis at pace and synthesise information and complex concepts into meaningful actions or proposals. Works collaboratively across all internal departments to harness skills and experience from across the business to deliver high quality services. Excellent interpersonal skills, the ability to work, and build relationships, with various stakeholders. Strong verbal and written communication skills, clearly and accurately presents information using appropriate language to construct persuasive arguments and points of advocacy that is easy to understand. Comfortable using AI tools to enhance productivity and decision making in day-to-day work. ‍ How to apply: To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. Closing date: Tuesday 30th June 9:00am

item.title: Policy Manager (Commercial Finance)
item.description: ‍ The organisation ‍ Ellwood Atfield is proud to be partnering with a leading trade association which represents the interests of members across a broad spectrum of financial services. Working closely with industry leaders, government, regulators and the media, the organisation champions the developments of a thriving sector which serves society and ensuring that the UK retains its role as a global leader in financial services. ‍ The opportunity ‍ The organisation is looking for a Policy Manager to join the Commercial Finance team at a significant moment for the sector with an unprecedented focus on business growth and access to finance. The role represents an exciting opportunity to influence the future regulatory frameworks around business lending and shape the narrative around high profile issues such as Covid loan schemes. ‍ As a Policy Manager, you will play a pivotal role in delivering against the organisation’s policy priorities in collaboration with its members - ranging from the largest UK retail banks to smaller and specialist lenders, as well as non-bank alternative lenders. ‍ Key Responsibilities ‍ Acting as subject matter expert on specific areas of policy such as the regulation of SME finance and government loan schemes. Providing expertise in areas including prudential regulation, smart data, restructuring and insolvency, economic crime and digital assets. Supporting the running of key member groups, including active participation at such meetings and deputising for senior colleagues. Supporting relationship management with members, associate members and key stakeholders (government departments, British Business Bank, Financial Conduct Authority, business groups and others). Preparing consultation responses and other advocacy, briefings, newsletter updates, blogs and presentations. Analysing data and economic insights to inform advocacy and provide actionable insights. Horizon scanning of developments such as matters that could impact Commercial Finance, identifying the relevance for members and proactively considering action. ‍ ‍ Key Skills ‍ Proven experience in financial services or a related sector, a trade body/member organisation, professional services or government/civil service/regulator. Experience of a relevant regulatory regime such as consumer credit or a proven ability to quickly gain proficiency in complex regulation. Strong attention to detail with an ability to communicate complex information in an accessible manner to a broad audience. Ability to identify key political developments and analyse their impact on members, whilst working in tandem with members to create appropriate policy responses. Strong research skills, ensuring policy proposals are supported by credible evidence. ‍ ‍ How to apply: To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. Closing date: Friday 3 rd July 9:00am

item.description: ‍ About the organisation: ‍ Ellwood Atfield is proud to be partnering with a respected regulatory organisation operating in the public interest. item.title: Senior Director, Corporate Communications (Europe)
item.description: The organisation: ‍ Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering exclusively with a global payments technology leader to find a Senior Director, Corporate Communications for Europe. Our client is operating at the heart of digital commerce and financial services, with a significant presence across European markets. This is an organisation that sits at the centre of major conversations shaping the future of payments, regulation, and economic growth. ‍ ‍ The opportunity: ‍ As Senior Director, Corporate Communications (Europe), you will lead integrated communications programmes that support business priorities and strengthen the organisation’s reputation across Europe. Working in close partnership with Government Affairs, Risk and Legal teams, you will drive corporate media, external relations, and reputation strategy across multiple markets. This role requires strong judgement in high-profile situations, the ability to operate across multiple European markets at pace, and a proven track record of advising senior leaders. ‍ The role will lead a small but high-performing team, manage agency relationships and budgets, and act as the primary point of contact for issues management and crisis communications across Europe. ‍ ‍ Experience required: ‍ Extensive experience in corporate communications, public affairs, and issues management Experience in payments, financial services, technology, or other regulated industries Strong track record advising senior executives in high-stakes situations Proven ability to lead communications across multiple European markets Excellent writing and communication skills with the ability to craft compelling narratives on complex issues Ability to operate at pace, manage multiple priorities, and work effectively in fast-moving environments Strong leadership, team management, and stakeholder management capability ‍ ‍ If you are a senior communications leader with a passion for high-impact work in a fast-moving, regulated industry, and the drive to shape reputation strategy at a European level, we look forward to hearing from you. ‍ ‍ Benefits: ‍ Office based in Central London ‍ How to apply: ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. item.title: Head of Policy & Standards
item.description: About the organisation: ‍ Ellwood Atfield is delighted to be partnering with the Royal College of Surgeons of England on the appointment of a Head of Policy & Standards. ‍ The Royal College of Surgeons of England is entering an exciting new phase in its policy, standards and influencing work, with the organisation evolving its Policy and Campaigns function to deliver greater impact across the issues affecting surgeons, dental professionals and patients. ‍ Representing over 30,000 surgeons and dental surgeons worldwide, the College plays a leading role in setting standards in surgical care, supporting the profession and shaping debate across major healthcare, workforce and regulatory challenges. ‍ As the College continues to strengthen its position as the leading voice of surgery, this role will play a critical part in ensuring its policy and standards work remains authoritative, evidence-based and influential. ‍ ‍ The opportunity: ‍ This is an outstanding opportunity for an experienced policy leader to shape the future of surgery and dentistry across the UK and internationally. ‍ Reporting to the Director of Communications and with four direct reports, the Head of Policy & Standards will be responsible for setting the strategic direction of the College's policy and standards agenda. The successful candidate will oversee the development of policy positions, professional guidance and standards that influence decision-makers, support the profession and ultimately improve patient care. ‍ The successful candidate will ensure policy and standards work translates into real-world impact, identifying emerging issues, generating evidence, responding rapidly to developments and working closely with colleagues across campaigns, communications and engagement to influence change. ‍ The role offers the opportunity to work closely with senior clinicians, healthcare leaders, regulators, policymakers and government stakeholders while helping shape the future direction of one of the UK's most respected professional bodies. ‍ ‍ Main responsibilities: ‍ Lead the strategic development of the College's policy and standards agenda across surgery and dentistry. Oversee horizon scanning to identify emerging political, clinical, workforce and regulatory developments. Translate complex evidence, data and expert insight into clear and influential policy positions and recommendations. Ensure policy development is evidence-based, academically rigorous and focused on delivering impact. Lead the development and governance of professional standards, guidance and frameworks. Build and maintain relationships with government departments, regulators, healthcare organisations, professional bodies and international partners. Represent the College in high-level policy discussions, consultations, inquiries and stakeholder forums. Work closely with campaigns, communications and engagement colleagues to maximise the impact of policy and standards activity. Provide strategic advice to senior leadership, Council and the Faculty of Dental Surgery Board. Lead, develop and support a high-performing Policy & Standards team. ‍ ‍ ‍ Person specification: ‍ Significant experience leading policy development within healthcare, government, regulation, membership organisations, professional bodies or related environments. Demonstrable experience translating complex evidence, data and stakeholder insight into clear policy recommendations. Strong understanding of healthcare policy, regulation, professional standards and workforce challenges. Experience engaging and influencing senior stakeholders, including policymakers, regulators, clinicians and healthcare leaders. Proven ability to develop strategic policy positions and deliver high-impact policy outputs. Experience managing teams and creating high-performing, collaborative working cultures. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including briefing senior leaders and external stakeholders. Strong analytical skills, sound judgement and the ability to operate effectively in a complex and fast-moving environment. ‍ ‍ How to apply: ‍ To apply, please submit the form below and include a copy of your CV. Closing date: Wednesday 15 July 2026 We will only be able to consider applications submitted via the online link attached and not sent via email. ‍ ‍ Ellwood Atfield is the leading headhunter for corporate affairs, communications, policy and advocacy.
